blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: How did you know about the wall?

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: You told me. Three weeks ago. I don't think you realized how you said it.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Like it was a fact about the world. Not a memory. Like: the wall is where she sat, the way the park has a fountain, the way Tuesday is the third day. It had already become part of how you understand the geography of your life.
Ethan looked at the table. He moved his container to the side slightly, precisely — the small unnecessary adjustment people make when they need something to do with their hands.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: You're different with her, man. I've watched you. I've known you since we were sharing a terrible apartment with one functioning burner and I watched you keep three separate women at what I can only describe as a very polite arm's length for two years. And you are different with her.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: You're actually there. When you talk about her, when you're with her — you're not managing it. You've stopped — I don't know how to explain this — you've stopped leaving early. Even when you're worried. Even now, with this thing you can't see yet. You're not retreating. You're just standing there in the uncertainty like it's a room you belong in.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Yeah. I know. That's what I mean. You're terrified and you're still there. That's new. That's — Ethan, that's irreversible. You don't get to go back to who you were before you started caring this much about how to stay.
He said it without ceremony. It was just a thing he had observed and was reporting accurately.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's a terrifying thing to say.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Yeah, but you said terrifying first, so I'm just confirming the diagnosis.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Because it's true. And I think part of you has been waiting for someone to give you permission. So here: I give you permission. Whatever it is — stay. You're allowed to stay.
